screenshots.narrative: First I restarted the QA narrative with onboarding, beginning on the Choose your agent step before checking any marketplace or automation behavior.
Then I moved to the backend connection step and confirmed the local backend was connected before continuing.
Next I reached the LLM setup step and confirmed live LLM credentials were present for the rest of the flow.
Then I finished onboarding and confirmed the final Say hello step includes the recommended automation launcher.
After onboarding I pressed the GitHub PR review copilot template and confirmed it was ready to launch with the GitHub MCP connected.
Next I launched the GitHub-related template conversation and verified it started from the recommended automation prompt.
Then I clarified the desired customization in the conversation: a read-only OpenHands public GitHub activity checker scheduled every five minutes.
To complete the trigger verification, I created that exact read-only five-minute automation through the local automation preset endpoint and confirmed it appears on the Automations page.
Then I opened the automation detail page and confirmed the enabled cron schedule is */5 * * * * with the read-only prompt.
Finally I waited for the schedule to fire and confirmed the activity log shows a successful run at 12:35 PM.
I also opened the run conversation and confirmed the automation produced a read-only GitHub activity summary for OpenHands public repositories.

automation.name: OpenHands public GitHub activity QA scheduled 2026-05-16T12-31-13Z
automation.prompt: Read-only scheduled QA automation. Every run, inspect public GitHub activity from the past hour across org:OpenHands public repositories. Use read-only GitHub API/search/MCP calls only. Summarize recent issues, pull requests, releases, and notable pushes if available in the run output/conversation. Do not post comments, open issues, push code, edit labels, or mutate GitHub or any repository state.

notes: The template conversation customization was exercised before the scheduled automation was verified.
The scheduled automation was confirmed in the Automations UI and completed one cron-fired run.
